1.description: Aggregate credit supply: Private credit to GDP, Baa-Aaa spread, and high-yield share of corporate debt issuance. The top panel shows a time-series plot of the private credit to GDP ratio. The bottom panel shows a time-series plot of the Baa-Aaa spread (left axis) and the high-yield share (HYS) of corporate debt issuance from Greenwood and Hanson (2013) (right axis). Shaded bars represent NBER recession dates. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Credit expansion in early and late deregulation states. This figure shows the average of total bank credit indices (normalized to 100 for each state in 1982) across early- and late-deregulation states. Total bank credit is the sum of household loans and commercial and industrial loans in the Call Reports. Early-deregulation states are defined as the states that deregulated intra- or interstate restrictions in 1983 or earlier, and late-deregulation states are states that began the deregulation process after 1983. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Household and firm credit shocks in the model. This figure presents comparative statics fir a household credit shock,θH, tradable firm credit shockθT, and nontradable firm credit shockθN. In each panel, the credit shock shifts the equilibrium from A to B. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Credit growth and deregulation. This figure presents estimates of{βy}fromyst=αs+αt+∑y≠19821t=ydsβy+εst, wheredsis the deregulation measure. Dashed lines represent 95% confidence intervals from standard errors clustered at the state level. Household loans are based on the Call Report items Loans to Individuals and Real Estate Loans. Commercial and industrial loans are based on the Call Report items Commercial and Industrial Loan. Consumer loans are based on the Call Report item Loans to Individuals and subsume home equity loans starting in 1987. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Deregulation and employment growth, 1982-1989. This figure presents scatterplots of the deregulation measure on employment growth by industry from 1982 to 1989. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Inflation and deregulation. This figure presents scatterplots of CPI inflation from 1982 (1984) to 1989 on the deregulation measure. The top right and bottom left panels show inflation for tradables (“Commodities”) and nontradables (“Services”) for 25 states for which this measure is available (excluding Alaska). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Deregulation and unemployment, real GDP per capita, house prices, and housing units. This figure presents estimates of{βq}fromYst=αs+γt+∑q≠19821t=q·DEREGs·βq+εst, whereDEREGsis the deregulation measure andYstis the state unemployment rate, log total employment, log real GDP per capita, log house prices, or log housing units. Dashed lines represent 95% confidence intervals from standard errors clustered at the state level. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Household credit boom and the subsequent recession. This figure presents scatterplots of the change in various outcomes from 1989 to 1992 against theΔ82−89HH leverage index. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Consumer prices and wages over the full cycle. This figure presents estimates of{βq}fromYst=αs+γt+∑q≠19821t=q·DEREGs·βq+εst, whereDEREGsis the deregulation measure. The dependent variable is the log of the state CPI for all items or a measure of residualized state wages from the CPS. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
